# Hono - Ultrafast Web Framework

Hono (meaning "flame" in Japanese) is a small, simple, and ultrafast web framework built on Web Standards. It works on any JavaScript runtime including Cloudflare Workers, Fastly Compute, Deno, Bun, Vercel, Netlify, AWS Lambda, Lambda@Edge, and Node.js. The framework is designed to be lightweight (under 14KB with the tiny preset), has zero dependencies, and provides first-class TypeScript support with excellent developer experience.

Hono provides a batteries-included approach with built-in middleware for common tasks like authentication, CORS, logging, and caching. The RPC feature enables type-safe client-server communication, allowing API specifications to be shared between server and client through TypeScript types. Combined with JSX support for server-side rendering, Hono can be used for building Web APIs, CDN edge applications, proxy servers, and full-stack applications.

## Creating a Hono Application

The `Hono` class is the primary object used to create web applications. It provides methods for routing, middleware registration, error handling, and request processing.

```typescript
import { Hono } from 'hono'

const app = new Hono()

// Basic route handlers
app.get('/', (c) => c.text('Hello Hono!'))
app.post('/posts', (c) => c.json({ message: 'Created!' }, 201))
app.put('/posts/:id', (c) => c.text(`Updated ${c.req.param('id')}`))
app.delete('/posts/:id', (c) => c.text('Deleted'))

// Custom 404 handler
app.notFound((c) => {
  return c.text('Custom 404 - Page Not Found', 404)
})

// Global error handler
app.onError((err, c) => {
  console.error(`${err}`)
  return c.text('Internal Server Error', 500)
})

export default app
```

## Routing with Path Parameters

Hono provides flexible routing with support for path parameters, optional parameters, wildcards, and regular expressions. Routes are matched in registration order.

```typescript
import { Hono } from 'hono'

const app = new Hono()

// Path parameters
app.get('/user/:name', (c) => {
  const name = c.req.param('name')
  return c.text(`Hello, ${name}!`)
})

// Multiple parameters
app.get('/posts/:id/comment/:commentId', (c) => {
  const { id, commentId } = c.req.param()
  return c.json({ postId: id, commentId })
})

// Optional parameter
app.get('/api/animal/:type?', (c) => {
  const type = c.req.param('type') || 'all'
  return c.text(`Animal type: ${type}`)
})

// Regex constraint
app.get('/post/:date{[0-9]+}/:title{[a-z]+}', (c) => {
  const { date, title } = c.req.param()
  return c.json({ date, title })
})

// Wildcard
app.get('/files/*', (c) => c.text('File route'))

// Multiple HTTP methods
app.on(['PUT', 'DELETE'], '/resource', (c) => c.text('PUT or DELETE'))

// Multiple paths
app.on('GET', ['/hello', '/hi', '/hey'], (c) => c.text('Greeting!'))

export default app
```

## Route Grouping

Group related routes using separate Hono instances and combine them with the `route()` method for better organization.

```typescript
import { Hono } from 'hono'

// Create sub-routers
const books = new Hono()
books.get('/', (c) => c.json({ books: ['Book 1', 'Book 2'] }))
books.get('/:id', (c) => c.json({ id: c.req.param('id'), title: 'Book Title' }))
books.post('/', (c) => c.json({ message: 'Book created' }, 201))

const authors = new Hono()
authors.get('/', (c) => c.json({ authors: ['Author 1', 'Author 2'] }))
authors.get('/:id', (c) => c.json({ id: c.req.param('id'), name: 'Author Name' }))

// Main app
const app = new Hono()

// Mount sub-routers
app.route('/books', books)     // /books, /books/:id
app.route('/authors', authors) // /authors, /authors/:id

// Using basePath for API versioning
const api = new Hono().basePath('/api/v1')
api.get('/users', (c) => c.json({ users: [] })) // /api/v1/users

export default app
```

## Context Object - Request and Response

The Context object (`c`) provides access to request data and methods for building responses. It includes helpers for JSON, text, HTML, redirects, and more.

```typescript
import { Hono } from 'hono'

const app = new Hono()

app.get('/api/data', (c) => {
  // Access request info
  const userAgent = c.req.header('User-Agent')
  const url = c.req.url
  const method = c.req.method
  const path = c.req.path

  // Query parameters
  const page = c.req.query('page')           // Single param
  const { q, limit } = c.req.query()         // All params
  const tags = c.req.queries('tags')         // Array: ?tags=a&tags=b

  // Set response headers
  c.header('X-Custom-Header', 'value')
  c.header('Cache-Control', 'max-age=3600')

  // Return JSON response
  return c.json({
    message: 'Success',
    userAgent,
    query: { page, q, limit, tags }
  })
})

// Different response types
app.get('/text', (c) => c.text('Plain text response'))
app.get('/html', (c) => c.html('<h1>HTML Response</h1>'))
app.get('/redirect', (c) => c.redirect('/new-location'))
app.get('/redirect-permanent', (c) => c.redirect('/new-location', 301))

// Custom status code
app.post('/create', (c) => {
  c.status(201)
  return c.json({ id: 1, created: true })
})

// Body with status and headers
app.get('/custom', (c) => {
  return c.body('Custom response', 200, {
    'Content-Type': 'text/plain',
    'X-Custom': 'Header value'
  })
})

export default app
```

## Parsing Request Body

Hono provides methods to parse different content types including JSON, form data, and multipart uploads.

```typescript
import { Hono } from 'hono'

const app = new Hono()

// JSON body (Content-Type: application/json)
app.post('/api/json', async (c) => {
  const body = await c.req.json()
  return c.json({ received: body })
})

// Form data (Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded or multipart/form-data)
app.post('/api/form', async (c) => {
  const body = await c.req.parseBody()
  const name = body['name']
  return c.text(`Hello, ${name}`)
})

// Multiple files with same field name
app.post('/api/upload', async (c) => {
  const body = await c.req.parseBody()
  const files = body['files[]']  // Array of files
  return c.json({ fileCount: Array.isArray(files) ? files.length : 1 })
})

// Dot notation for nested form data
app.post('/api/nested', async (c) => {
  const body = await c.req.parseBody({ dot: true })
  // Input: obj.key1=value1&obj.key2=value2
  // Result: { obj: { key1: 'value1', key2: 'value2' } }
  return c.json(body)
})

// Text body
app.post('/api/text', async (c) => {
  const text = await c.req.text()
  return c.text(`Received: ${text}`)
})

// Raw binary data
app.post('/api/binary', async (c) => {
  const buffer = await c.req.arrayBuffer()
  return c.json({ size: buffer.byteLength })
})

export default app
```

## Middleware

Middleware functions execute before/after route handlers, enabling request preprocessing, response modification, and cross-cutting concerns.

```typescript
import { Hono } from 'hono'
import { logger } from 'hono/logger'
import { cors } from 'hono/cors'
import { basicAuth } from 'hono/basic-auth'
import { createMiddleware } from 'hono/factory'

const app = new Hono()

// Built-in middleware
app.use(logger())  // Log all requests
app.use('/api/*', cors())  // CORS for API routes

// Basic authentication for protected routes
app.use('/admin/*', basicAuth({
  username: 'admin',
  password: 'secret'
}))

// Custom middleware inline
app.use(async (c, next) => {
  const start = Date.now()
  await next()
  const ms = Date.now() - start
  c.header('X-Response-Time', `${ms}ms`)
})

// Custom middleware with factory (type-safe)
const authMiddleware = createMiddleware<{
  Variables: { userId: string }
}>(async (c, next) => {
  const token = c.req.header('Authorization')
  if (!token) {
    return c.json({ error: 'Unauthorized' }, 401)
  }
  c.set('userId', 'user-123')  // Set variable for handlers
  await next()
})

// Apply custom middleware to specific route
app.get('/profile', authMiddleware, (c) => {
  const userId = c.get('userId')  // Access set variable
  return c.json({ userId, profile: 'data' })
})

// Middleware that modifies response
app.use('/api/*', async (c, next) => {
  await next()
  c.res.headers.set('X-API-Version', '1.0')
})

app.get('/admin/dashboard', (c) => c.text('Admin Dashboard'))
app.get('/api/users', (c) => c.json({ users: [] }))

export default app
```

## CORS Middleware

Configure Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) to control which origins can access your API.

```typescript
import { Hono } from 'hono'
import { cors } from 'hono/cors'

const app = new Hono()

// Basic CORS - allow all origins
app.use('/api/*', cors())

// Configured CORS
app.use('/api/v2/*', cors({
  origin: 'https://example.com',
  allowHeaders: ['X-Custom-Header', 'Content-Type'],
  allowMethods: ['POST', 'GET', 'OPTIONS', 'DELETE'],
  exposeHeaders: ['Content-Length', 'X-Request-Id'],
  maxAge: 600,
  credentials: true,
}))

// Multiple origins
app.use('/api/v3/*', cors({
  origin: ['https://app.example.com', 'https://admin.example.com'],
}))

// Dynamic origin validation
app.use('/api/v4/*', cors({
  origin: (origin, c) => {
    return origin.endsWith('.example.com') ? origin : 'https://example.com'
  },
}))

// Environment-based CORS
app.use('*', async (c, next) => {
  const corsMiddleware = cors({
    origin: c.env.CORS_ORIGIN,  // From environment variable
  })
  return corsMiddleware(c, next)
})

app.get('/api/data', (c) => c.json({ data: 'value' }))

export default app
```

## JWT Authentication

Protect routes with JWT (JSON Web Token) authentication using the built-in JWT middleware.

```typescript
import { Hono } from 'hono'
import { jwt } from 'hono/jwt'
import { sign } from 'hono/jwt'
import type { JwtVariables } from 'hono/jwt'

type Variables = JwtVariables

const app = new Hono<{ Variables: Variables }>()

const JWT_SECRET = 'your-secret-key'

// Public route - generate token
app.post('/login', async (c) => {
  const { username, password } = await c.req.json()

  // Validate credentials (simplified)
  if (username === 'user' && password === 'pass') {
    const payload = {
      sub: username,
      role: 'user',
      exp: Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000) + 60 * 60, // 1 hour
    }
    const token = await sign(payload, JWT_SECRET)
    return c.json({ token })
  }

  return c.json({ error: 'Invalid credentials' }, 401)
})

// Protected routes - require JWT
app.use('/api/*', jwt({
  secret: JWT_SECRET,
  alg: 'HS256',
}))

// Access JWT payload in protected routes
app.get('/api/profile', (c) => {
  const payload = c.get('jwtPayload')
  return c.json({
    user: payload.sub,
    role: payload.role
  })
})

// JWT with environment variable
app.use('/secure/*', (c, next) => {
  const jwtMiddleware = jwt({
    secret: c.env.JWT_SECRET,
    alg: 'HS256',
  })
  return jwtMiddleware(c, next)
})

export default app
```

## Basic Authentication

Implement HTTP Basic Authentication for simple password protection.

```typescript
import { Hono } from 'hono'
import { basicAuth } from 'hono/basic-auth'

const app = new Hono()

// Simple basic auth
app.use('/admin/*', basicAuth({
  username: 'admin',
  password: 'secretpassword',
}))

// Multiple users
app.use('/team/*', basicAuth(
  { username: 'alice', password: 'alice123', realm: 'Team Area' },
  { username: 'bob', password: 'bob456' },
  { username: 'charlie', password: 'charlie789' }
))

// Custom verification function
app.use('/custom/*', basicAuth({
  verifyUser: async (username, password, c) => {
    // Check against database or external service
    const user = await validateUser(username, password)
    return user !== null
  },
}))

// With success callback to set context variables
app.use('/auth/*', basicAuth({
  username: 'user',
  password: 'pass',
  onAuthSuccess: (c, username) => {
    c.set('authenticatedUser', username)
  },
}))

app.get('/auth/whoami', (c) => {
  const user = c.get('authenticatedUser')
  return c.text(`Logged in as: ${user}`)
})

app.get('/admin/dashboard', (c) => c.text('Admin Dashboard'))

async function validateUser(username: string, password: string) {
  // Implement your validation logic
  return username === 'valid' && password === 'valid' ? { id: 1 } : null
}

export default app
```

## Validation with Zod

Validate request data using Zod schemas with the Zod Validator middleware for type-safe input handling.

```typescript
import { Hono } from 'hono'
import { zValidator } from '@hono/zod-validator'
import { z } from 'zod'

const app = new Hono()

// JSON body validation
const createUserSchema = z.object({
  name: z.string().min(1),
  email: z.string().email(),
  age: z.number().min(0).optional(),
})

app.post('/users',
  zValidator('json', createUserSchema),
  (c) => {
    const data = c.req.valid('json')
    // data is typed as { name: string, email: string, age?: number }
    return c.json({ id: 1, ...data }, 201)
  }
)

// Query parameter validation
const searchSchema = z.object({
  q: z.string().min(1),
  page: z.coerce.number().min(1).default(1),
  limit: z.coerce.number().min(1).max(100).default(10),
})

app.get('/search',
  zValidator('query', searchSchema),
  (c) => {
    const { q, page, limit } = c.req.valid('query')
    return c.json({ query: q, page, limit, results: [] })
  }
)

// Path parameter validation
const idSchema = z.object({
  id: z.string().uuid(),
})

app.get('/users/:id',
  zValidator('param', idSchema),
  (c) => {
    const { id } = c.req.valid('param')
    return c.json({ id, name: 'User' })
  }
)

// Multiple validators
app.put('/users/:id',
  zValidator('param', idSchema),
  zValidator('json', createUserSchema.partial()),
  (c) => {
    const { id } = c.req.valid('param')
    const updates = c.req.valid('json')
    return c.json({ id, ...updates })
  }
)

// Custom error handling
app.post('/strict',
  zValidator('json', createUserSchema, (result, c) => {
    if (!result.success) {
      return c.json({
        error: 'Validation failed',
        details: result.error.issues
      }, 400)
    }
  }),
  (c) => {
    const data = c.req.valid('json')
    return c.json(data)
  }
)

export default app
```

## RPC - Type-Safe Client

Hono's RPC feature enables end-to-end type safety between server and client, sharing API specifications through TypeScript types.

```typescript
// server.ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'
import { zValidator } from '@hono/zod-validator'
import { z } from 'zod'

const app = new Hono()

const route = app
  .get('/posts', (c) => {
    return c.json({
      posts: [
        { id: 1, title: 'Hello' },
        { id: 2, title: 'World' },
      ]
    }, 200)
  })
  .post('/posts',
    zValidator('json', z.object({
      title: z.string(),
      body: z.string(),
    })),
    (c) => {
      const { title, body } = c.req.valid('json')
      return c.json({ id: 3, title, body, created: true }, 201)
    }
  )
  .get('/posts/:id', (c) => {
    const id = c.req.param('id')
    return c.json({ id, title: 'Post Title', body: 'Content' }, 200)
  })
  .delete('/posts/:id', (c) => {
    const id = c.req.param('id')
    return c.json({ deleted: true, id }, 200)
  })

export type AppType = typeof route
export default app

// client.ts
import { hc } from 'hono/client'
import type { AppType } from './server'
import type { InferRequestType, InferResponseType } from 'hono/client'

// Create typed client
const client = hc<AppType>('http://localhost:8787')

// GET request
const listPosts = async () => {
  const res = await client.posts.$get()
  if (res.ok) {
    const data = await res.json()
    console.log(data.posts) // Typed as { id: number, title: string }[]
  }
}

// POST request with typed body
const createPost = async () => {
  const res = await client.posts.$post({
    json: {
      title: 'New Post',
      body: 'Post content',
    }
  })
  if (res.ok) {
    const data = await res.json()
    console.log(data.id, data.created)
  }
}

// GET with path parameter
const getPost = async (id: string) => {
  const res = await client.posts[':id'].$get({
    param: { id }
  })
  const data = await res.json()
  return data
}

// Infer types for external use
type CreatePostRequest = InferRequestType<typeof client.posts.$post>['json']
type PostResponse = InferResponseType<typeof client.posts.$get, 200>
```

## Cookie Helper

Manage HTTP cookies with support for signed cookies, secure prefixes, and configurable options.

```typescript
import { Hono } from 'hono'
import {
  getCookie,
  setCookie,
  deleteCookie,
  getSignedCookie,
  setSignedCookie
} from 'hono/cookie'

const app = new Hono()

const COOKIE_SECRET = 'my-secret-key-for-signing'

// Set and get cookies
app.get('/cookie/set', (c) => {
  setCookie(c, 'session', 'abc123', {
    path: '/',
    secure: true,
    httpOnly: true,
    maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24, // 1 day
    sameSite: 'Lax',
  })
  return c.text('Cookie set!')
})

app.get('/cookie/get', (c) => {
  const session = getCookie(c, 'session')
  const allCookies = getCookie(c)
  return c.json({ session, allCookies })
})

app.get('/cookie/delete', (c) => {
  deleteCookie(c, 'session', { path: '/' })
  return c.text('Cookie deleted!')
})

// Signed cookies (tamper-proof)
app.get('/signed/set', async (c) => {
  await setSignedCookie(c, 'user_id', '12345', COOKIE_SECRET, {
    path: '/',
    httpOnly: true,
  })
  return c.text('Signed cookie set!')
})

app.get('/signed/get', async (c) => {
  const userId = await getSignedCookie(c, COOKIE_SECRET, 'user_id')
  if (userId === false) {
    return c.text('Cookie was tampered with!', 400)
  }
  return c.json({ userId })
})

// Secure prefix cookies
app.get('/secure-prefix', (c) => {
  setCookie(c, 'token', 'value', {
    prefix: 'secure',  // Adds __Secure- prefix
    secure: true,
    path: '/',
  })
  return c.text('Secure prefix cookie set!')
})

export default app
```

## Streaming Responses

Send streaming responses for real-time data, Server-Sent Events (SSE), and large file transfers.

```typescript
import { Hono } from 'hono'
import { stream, streamText, streamSSE } from 'hono/streaming'

const app = new Hono()

// Basic streaming
app.get('/stream', (c) => {
  return stream(c, async (stream) => {
    stream.onAbort(() => {
      console.log('Client disconnected')
    })

    await stream.write(new Uint8Array([72, 101, 108, 108, 111])) // "Hello"
    await stream.write(new Uint8Array([32, 87, 111, 114, 108, 100])) // " World"
  })
})

// Text streaming
app.get('/stream-text', (c) => {
  return streamText(c, async (stream) => {
    await stream.writeln('Starting process...')
    await stream.sleep(1000)
    await stream.writeln('Step 1 complete')
    await stream.sleep(1000)
    await stream.writeln('Step 2 complete')
    await stream.sleep(1000)
    await stream.write('Done!')
  })
})

// Server-Sent Events (SSE)
app.get('/sse', (c) => {
  return streamSSE(c, async (stream) => {
    let id = 0
    while (true) {
      await stream.writeSSE({
        data: JSON.stringify({
          time: new Date().toISOString(),
          message: 'Heartbeat'
        }),
        event: 'heartbeat',
        id: String(id++),
      })
      await stream.sleep(1000)
    }
  })
})

// Streaming with error handling
app.get('/stream-safe', (c) => {
  return stream(
    c,
    async (stream) => {
      await stream.write(new TextEncoder().encode('Data chunk 1\n'))
      await stream.sleep(500)
      await stream.write(new TextEncoder().encode('Data chunk 2\n'))
    },
    (err, stream) => {
      console.error('Stream error:', err)
      stream.writeln('An error occurred')
    }
  )
})

export default app
```

## JSX Server-Side Rendering

Render HTML using JSX syntax with Hono's built-in JSX support for server-side rendering.

```tsx
// Requires tsconfig.json: { "compilerOptions": { "jsx": "react-jsx", "jsxImportSource": "hono/jsx" } }

import { Hono } from 'hono'
import type { FC } from 'hono/jsx'

const app = new Hono()

// Layout component
const Layout: FC<{ title: string }> = (props) => {
  return (
    <html>
      <head>
        <title>{props.title}</title>
        <meta charset="UTF-8" />
      </head>
      <body>{props.children}</body>
    </html>
  )
}

// Page component
const HomePage: FC<{ user: string; items: string[] }> = (props) => {
  return (
    <Layout title="Home">
      <h1>Welcome, {props.user}!</h1>
      <ul>
        {props.items.map((item, i) => (
          <li key={i}>{item}</li>
        ))}
      </ul>
    </Layout>
  )
}

// Async component
const AsyncData: FC = async () => {
  const data = await fetchData()
  return <div>Loaded: {data}</div>
}

async function fetchData() {
  await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 100))
  return 'Dynamic content'
}

// Routes
app.get('/', (c) => {
  return c.html(
    <HomePage
      user="Guest"
      items={['Item 1', 'Item 2', 'Item 3']}
    />
  )
})

app.get('/async', (c) => {
  return c.html(
    <Layout title="Async">
      <AsyncData />
    </Layout>
  )
})

// Using setRenderer for consistent layouts
app.use('*', async (c, next) => {
  c.setRenderer((content) => {
    return c.html(
      <html>
        <head><title>My App</title></head>
        <body>
          <nav>Navigation</nav>
          <main>{content}</main>
          <footer>Footer</footer>
        </body>
      </html>
    )
  })
  await next()
})

app.get('/page', (c) => {
  return c.render(<h1>Page Content</h1>)
})

export default app
```

## Testing

Test Hono applications using the built-in `app.request()` method with any testing framework like Vitest or Jest.

```typescript
import { Hono } from 'hono'
import { describe, test, expect } from 'vitest'

const app = new Hono()

app.get('/hello', (c) => c.text('Hello World'))
app.get('/json', (c) => c.json({ message: 'Success' }))
app.post('/posts', async (c) => {
  const body = await c.req.json()
  return c.json({ id: 1, ...body }, 201)
})
app.get('/user/:id', (c) => c.json({ id: c.req.param('id') }))

// Tests
describe('API Tests', () => {
  test('GET /hello returns text', async () => {
    const res = await app.request('/hello')
    expect(res.status).toBe(200)
    expect(await res.text()).toBe('Hello World')
  })

  test('GET /json returns JSON', async () => {
    const res = await app.request('/json')
    expect(res.status).toBe(200)
    expect(await res.json()).toEqual({ message: 'Success' })
  })

  test('POST /posts creates resource', async () => {
    const res = await app.request('/posts', {
      method: 'POST',
      body: JSON.stringify({ title: 'Test Post' }),
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    })
    expect(res.status).toBe(201)
    expect(await res.json()).toEqual({ id: 1, title: 'Test Post' })
  })

  test('GET /user/:id with path parameter', async () => {
    const res = await app.request('/user/123')
    expect(res.status).toBe(200)
    expect(await res.json()).toEqual({ id: '123' })
  })

  test('with mocked environment', async () => {
    const appWithEnv = new Hono<{ Bindings: { API_KEY: string } }>()
    appWithEnv.get('/api', (c) => c.json({ key: c.env.API_KEY }))

    const res = await appWithEnv.request('/api', {}, { API_KEY: 'test-key' })
    expect(await res.json()).toEqual({ key: 'test-key' })
  })
})
```

## Logger Middleware

Add request logging to track incoming requests and outgoing responses with timing information.

```typescript
import { Hono } from 'hono'
import { logger } from 'hono/logger'

const app = new Hono()

// Basic logger
app.use(logger())

// Custom logger function
const customLogger = (message: string, ...rest: string[]) => {
  console.log(`[${new Date().toISOString()}]`, message, ...rest)
}

app.use(logger(customLogger))

// Log output example:
// <-- GET /api/users
// --> GET /api/users 200 12ms

app.get('/api/users', (c) => c.json({ users: [] }))

export default app
```

## Factory Helper for Type-Safe Middleware

Use the Factory Helper to create reusable, type-safe middleware and handlers with proper TypeScript inference.

```typescript
import { Hono } from 'hono'
import { createFactory, createMiddleware } from 'hono/factory'

// Define environment types
type Env = {
  Bindings: { DATABASE_URL: string }
  Variables: {
    user: { id: string; name: string }
    db: { query: (sql: string) => Promise<any> }
  }
}

// Create factory with types
const factory = createFactory<Env>()

// Create typed middleware
const authMiddleware = factory.createMiddleware(async (c, next) => {
  const token = c.req.header('Authorization')
  if (!token) {
    return c.json({ error: 'Unauthorized' }, 401)
  }
  c.set('user', { id: '1', name: 'Alice' })
  await next()
})

const dbMiddleware = factory.createMiddleware(async (c, next) => {
  c.set('db', {
    query: async (sql) => ({ rows: [] })
  })
  await next()
})

// Create handlers with middleware
const handlers = factory.createHandlers(
  authMiddleware,
  dbMiddleware,
  (c) => {
    const user = c.var.user  // Typed: { id: string, name: string }
    const db = c.var.db      // Typed: { query: ... }
    return c.json({ user })
  }
)

// Create app with factory
const app = factory.createApp()

app.get('/profile', ...handlers)

// Standalone typed middleware
const timerMiddleware = createMiddleware<{
  Variables: { startTime: number }
}>(async (c, next) => {
  c.set('startTime', Date.now())
  await next()
  const elapsed = Date.now() - c.get('startTime')
  c.header('X-Response-Time', `${elapsed}ms`)
})

app.use(timerMiddleware)

export default app
```

## Summary

Hono excels as a versatile web framework for building APIs, edge applications, and full-stack applications across multiple JavaScript runtimes. Its core strengths include an expressive routing system with middleware composition, type-safe request validation using Zod, and end-to-end type safety through the RPC client. The framework's lightweight footprint (under 14KB) combined with built-in support for authentication, CORS, cookies, streaming, and JSX rendering makes it suitable for projects ranging from simple APIs to complex microservices.

Common integration patterns include using Hono with Cloudflare Workers for edge computing, combining it with D1/KV for serverless databases, deploying to platforms like Vercel/Netlify for JAMstack applications, and building type-safe monorepos where server and client share API types. The framework's adherence to Web Standards ensures portability across runtimes, while features like the Factory Helper and createMiddleware enable building reusable, well-typed middleware libraries that can be shared across projects.
